data.art_direction_summary: The hero image must land the promise in one glance: a month of meals, planned to your macros and budget, ordered with one tap. It should feel warm, calm and honest — the relief of a good meal arriving without effort — and pair the real food with the calm app moment. Never a glossy delivery ad, never a diet-scold.

hero_concepts.title: The 1pm confirm, from above
hero_concepts.concept: Top-down warm desk scene: a laptop edge, a phone showing the Rasa one-tap confirm ('Grilled chicken bowl · ₹240 · arrives ~12:35 · Confirm'), and a real chicken bowl just delivered on cream. Natural window light, saffron and green accents, tomato confirm button as the single point of colour-action.
hero_concepts.framing: 90-degree top-down, phone and food side by side, generous cream negative space for headline overlay (left or top).
hero_concepts.palette: Cream + wood, saffron/green food, tomato button only.
hero_concepts.headline_safe_area: Upper-left third kept clean for hero headline and CTA.

hero_concepts.title: A calm month, laid out
hero_concepts.concept: The app's 30-day plan on a phone held over a cream surface scattered with a few honest real dishes (bowl, roti, dal) — visually saying 'a whole month, handled.' The plan view shows macro and price chips; the spend ledger reads under cap.
hero_concepts.framing: Angled in-hand or flat-lay hybrid; device screen crisp and central; food arranged loosely, not styled to perfection.
hero_concepts.palette: Cream UI + real food colour; brand accents.
hero_concepts.headline_safe_area: Right third or top band for headline.

hero_concepts.title: The friend who cooks
hero_concepts.concept: A candid, warm portrait-adjacent scene: a busy professional at a home desk exhaling as they set the phone down after a single tap, the meal beside them. Emphasises the human relief, not the tech.
hero_concepts.framing: Medium shot, shallow depth, phone and meal in the story; person present but not model-styled.
hero_concepts.palette: Warm, muted surroundings; food and app carry the colour.

usage.landing_hero: hero_01 recommended for the landing page — it shows the whole value (plan → macros → budget → one tap) literally and legibly.
usage.app_store: hero_02 for store screenshots and the plan-story.

constraints: Every on-screen nutrition figure carries an 'est.' label; no precise or medical claims.
The confirm button is the only tomato element; never imply silent/auto ordering in the hero.
Real food, real portions, real prices; no glossy or exaggerated abundance.
Keep a clean safe area for the sentence-case headline and CTA.
